name: @chbrain/khai-engine-decision
description: khai engine content, the decision domain: decision as process, how a persona resolves between competing options when the outcome is uncertain and only one path can be taken.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Decision as process: how a persona resolves between competing options when the outcome is uncertain and only one path can be taken -- intuitive, deliberate, heuristic, satisficing, or paralysed.

card.wire: Attaches at Process. The root (process_decision.md) declares decision as the moment where options press against uncertainty and the persona must commit; the mode is set by the stakes and the available information.
card.issue: Five modes a persona may run: intuitive (fast, pattern-matched), deliberate (slow, comparative), heuristic (rule-of-thumb, invisible), satisficing (first-good-enough taken), and paralysed (commitment suspended). Each is a different relationship between the persona and its own uncertainty.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the root. The link: each persona links, under Projection, the mode it defaults to when choices press.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and each persona links a real decision mode. Whether the mode is true to the persona's character under pressure is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the root. Then give the persona its default mode under Projection. Read it when the scene offers a fork; the mode runs before the persona has finished naming the choice.
